# MMbook

> An immersive Markdown reader built with Tauri 2 + Svelte 5. Focused on the reading experience itself — spotlight focus mode, paper-themed UI, and lightning-fast startup.

MMbook is a desktop Markdown reader (not an editor) for individual developers and writers who want a calm, beautiful place to read .md files. It is built on Tauri 2 (Rust backend) with a Svelte 5 + TypeScript frontend, and is distributed as a small (<10MB) installer for Windows, macOS, and Linux.

## What makes MMbook different

- **Spotlight Focus Mode**: A three-tier opacity gradient (100% / 40% / 15%) that highlights only the paragraph you are currently reading. Inactive paragraphs gracefully dim with a 300ms transition.
- **Three theme pairs**: 素纸 (Plain Paper), 墨石 (Ink Stone), 暮光 (Twilight) — each with light + dark variants, designed for long reading sessions.
- **Floating global search**: macOS-style rounded search box with real-time highlight and jump-to-match.
- **Smart TOC**: Auto-extracted from Markdown heading levels, one-click navigation in the sidebar.
- **Inline editing**: Double-click any paragraph to edit in place; great for fixing typos without leaving the reader.
- **Performance**: Built with Rust + Tauri for millisecond cold start and minimal memory footprint.
- **Code highlighting**: Powered by Shiki for accurate, multi-language syntax highlighting.

## Links

- Product site: https://mmbook.vercel.app/
- Source code: https://github.com/lingcang728/MMbook
- Downloads: https://github.com/lingcang728/MMbook/releases
- Issues / feedback: https://github.com/lingcang728/MMbook/issues

## Stack

- Frontend: Svelte 5 + TypeScript + Vite
- Backend: Rust + Tauri 2.0
- Markdown engine: unified / remark / rehype
- Code highlighter: Shiki
- Storage: SQLite via Tauri SQL plugin (where applicable)

## Author

凌苍 (LingCang) — independent developer.
